initial_program:
% ----- Determine the maximal colour -----
maxc(K) :- K = #max{Color : color(Color), Color != 0}.

% ----- Find rows and columns that contain the maximal colour -----
maxcRow(R) :- input(R,_,maxc).
maxcCol(C) :- input(_,C,maxc).

% ----- Determine bounding rectangle of the maximal colour -----
% rows that contain the maximal colour
minR(K) :- K = #min{R : maxcRow(R)}.
maxR(K) :- K = #max{R : maxcRow(R)}.
% columns that contain the maximal colour
minC(K) :- K = #min{C : maxcCol(C)}.
maxC(K) :- K = #max{C : maxcCol(C)}.

% ----- Determine the size of the interior rectangle -----
% For rows we always remove the two extreme rows that contain the max colour
rowStart(N) :- N = minR(K) + 1.
rowEnd(N)  :- N = maxR(K) - 1.
Rdim(K) :- R = rowEnd(K) - rowStart(K) + 1.    % number of interior rows (>=0)

% For columns we remove the extreme columns only if the maximal colour appears in at least two different columns
colCount(K) :- K = #count{C : maxcCol(C)}.
colCount(K) = 1 : colInclusive(K).   % true when the maximal colour occurs in exactly one column
colCount(K) = 2 : colInclusive(K) :- colCount(K) = 1. % true when it occurs in two columns (the typical case)
colInclusive(K) :- colCount(K) = 2.   % colInclusive true means we keep the interior (exclusive) range; otherwise we keep the whole range (inclusive)

% Determine column boundaries
% If colInclusive(K) is true we keep the full range, otherwise we remove the extremes
colStart(K) :- colInclusive(K)  minC(K) : minC(K) + 1.
colEnd(K)   :- colInclusive(K)  maxC(K) : maxC(K) - 1.
Cdim(K) :- C = colEnd(K) - colStart(K) + 1.   % number of interior columns (>=0)

% ----- Compute central row and column (integer division) -----
centralRowOrig(K) :- CR = (rowStart(K) + rowEnd(K)) // 2.
centralColOrig(K) :- CC = (colStart(K) + colEnd(K)) // 2.

% ----- Interior indices for a cell -----
% For a cell (R,C) that will become output(R,C,Color) we define interior indices i,j
interiorRow(I)  :- R = rowStart(K) + I.
interiorCol(J)  :- C = colStart(K) + J.
i(J)  :- J = C - colStart(K).
i(R)  :- I = R - rowStart(K).

% ----- Define helper variables -----
i(I) :- I = R - rowStart(K).   % i for given interior row index
j(J) :- J = C - colStart(K).   % j for given interior column index

% ----- Compute middle indices -----
% middleRowIdx = (rowStart + rowEnd) // 2 - rowStart
middleRowIdx(K) :- MI = (rowStart(K) + rowEnd(K)) // 2 - rowStart(K).
% middleColIdx = (colStart + colEnd) // 2 - colStart
middleColIdx(K) :- MC = (colStart(K) + colEnd(K)) // 2 - colStart(K).

% ----- Helper offsets -----
offset(K) :- O = (middleColIdx(K) > middleRowIdx(K)  1 : 0).

% ----- Conditions that indicate a cell must be maxc -----
% 1. Central row (interior middle row) with columns from 1 to middleCol (inclusive)
centralRow(I,J) :- I = middleRowIdx(K), J >= 1, J <= middleColIdx(K).

% 2. Central column (interior middle column) with rows from middleRowIdx to Rdim-2 (inclusive)
centralCol(I,J) :- J = middleColIdx(K), I >= middleRowIdx(K), I <= Rdim(K) - 2.

% 3. Diagonal: i + j = Rdim + Cdim - 3 and i > middleRowIdx
diag(I,J) :- I + J = Rdim(K) + Cdim(K) - 3, I > middleRowIdx(K).

% 4. Bottom row (interior last row) with columns from middleColIdx (or middleColIdx+1) up to Cdim-2
bottomRow(I,J) :- I = Rdim(K) - 1, J != 0, J != Cdim(K) - 1, J >= middleColIdx(K) + O.

% 5. Left column at bottom row (interior column 0) when middleCol > middleRow
leftCol(I,J) :- J = 0, I = Rdim(K) - 1, I > middleRowIdx(K), middleColIdx(K) > middleRowIdx(K).

% 6. Right column (interior last column) with rows from middleRowIdx up to Rdim-2
rightCol(I,J) :- J = Cdim(K) - 1, I != 0, I <= Rdim(K) - 2, I >= middleRowIdx(K).

% ----- The condition that a cell must be coloured with the maximal colour -----
needMax(I,J) :- 
    centralRow(I,J) ; 
    centralCol(I,J) ; 
    diag(I,J) ; 
    bottomRow(I,J) ; 
    leftCol(I,J) ; 
    rightCol(I,J).

% ----- Build the output grid -----
% Output dimensions: rows 0..Rdim-1, columns 0..Cdim-1
row(0..Rdim(K)-1) :- true.
col(0..Cdim(K)-1) :- true.
output_cell(I,J) :- row(I), col(J).

% ----- Ensure exactly one colour per output cell -----
% For each output cell we guess its colour (either maxc or 0)
% We will enforce that cells with needMax must be maxc and others zero via constraints
1 { output(I,J,maxc) } 1 :- output_cell(I,J).

% For any interior cell where needMax is false, output must be zero
:- not needMax(I,J), output(I,J,maxc).

% For any interior cell where needMax is true, output must be maxc
:- needMax(I,J), output(I,J,0).

% Hide auxiliary predicates
#show output/3.

i(J)  :- J = C - colStart(K).

% 6. Right column (interior last column) with rows from middleRowIdx up to Rdim-2
